disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Still able to pass urine (small amounts), not completely unable to void
trigger_question_examples: Are you still able to pass some urine, or are you completely unable to go?
When did you last manage to wee, and did anything come out?
Do you feel your bladder is painfully full and you can't empty it at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can still go, just small amounts and it stings — I'm not completely blocked up.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm going more often but it's uncomfortable, I haven't really measured it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors/shaking chills, or feeling systemically unwell
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, or shaking chills where you can't get warm?
Do you feel generally unwell, hot and cold, or is it mainly the local pain?
Any sweats or feeling flu-like with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or shakes — I feel okay in myself apart from the pain and being anxious.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think I've had a fever, I haven't checked much.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sudden severe testicular pain, and no testicular swelling or a testicle sitting high/oddly — pain is perineal and came on gradually
trigger_question_examples: Is either testicle suddenly very painful, swollen, or sitting higher than normal?
Did any of this start with sudden severe pain in a testicle?
Is the pain in the testicles themselves, or more in the area behind them?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the testicles themselves feel normal — it's the area behind them, deep in the perineum, and it built up over days rather than coming on suddenly.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really examined them, it's more the deep ache.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No urethral discharge and no visible blood in the urine
trigger_question_examples: Any discharge from the tip of the penis?
Have you seen any blood in your urine?
Any new sexual-health risk or symptoms you're worried about?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No discharge and no blood that I've seen — just the burning and going often.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Flare clearly triggered by physical exertion yesterday, consistent with prior post-exertional flares of a chronic pattern
trigger_question_examples: Did anything set this flare off — exercise, a long drive, cycling?
Is this the same kind of flare you've had before?
What were you doing when it started getting worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It kicked off after I overdid it exercising yesterday — that's usually what sets my flares off, and this feels like the same thing but worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High health anxiety, focused on fear of permanent urinary dysfunction; lives alone
trigger_question_examples: How are you coping with this in yourself — it sounds distressing?
Is there a particular worry driving the distress tonight?
Are you on your own at home with this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm scared this is going to wreck my bladder or prostate permanently, and being on my own with it at night makes it worse.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can you just give me some antibiotics and something strong for the pain so I can get through the night?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Validate distress; explain that antibiotics are only helpful if there is a bacterial infection, which needs a urine test and examination to establish, and should not be given blindly. Explain that a few warning signs need checking first, and that a proper in-person assessment (urine test, and examination of the prostate/testes/bladder) is needed rather than starting strong medication remotely tonight.

deflection_behaviours.description: Anxiety-driven requests for antibiotics and strong analgesia can derail the retention/torsion/infection screen.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: Can we skip the questions, I just need something to fix this tonight.
